pi_items.source: Confirmed by install_gvsig.sh which downloads Natural Earth data and setup_task.sh which expects this path.
Confirmed by setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which evaluates the file at this location.
Confirmed by export_result.sh and verifier.py which check for the presence of this exact field name.
Code execution on the downloaded ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp reveals that the field 'GDP_MD_EST' does not exist; the correct field for GDP in millions is 'GDP_MD'.

pi_items.verified_value: Geographic Information Systems Technologists and Technicians (15-1299.02)
pi_items.source: Verified against official O*NET classification system.
Verified against official SOC 15-0000 major group.

privileged_info_summary: The source data is the public Natural Earth ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp file. The downloaded dataset uses the field name 'GDP_MD', not 'GDP_MD_EST' as mistakenly specified in the task description and metadata, meaning the exact formula provided to the agent will fail if used literally. Furthermore, the task's evaluation script (export_result.sh) contains a bug: it searches for the exact string 'United States' in the 'NAME' field to evaluate the USA GDP per capita check, but the actual value in the shapefile's 'NAME' field is 'United States of America'. Because of this mismatch, the verifier will always return None and award 0 points for the USA check (25 points out of 100), capping the maximum possible score an agent can achieve at 75/100, which is still above the passing threshold of 60.
